content: Just as language learners use the lexical concept of reflexion (reflecting on past writing and vocabulary use to correct missteps) to improve their second language output, large language models can leverage a hierarchical two-layer self-improvement framework that combines the episodic memory of past failure cases from Reflexion agents with the iterative step-by-step output polishing of Self-Refine to outperform either standalone method on complex reasoning tasks. This framework mirrors how human learners cycle between global reflection on past mistakes and targeted refinement of current work to build mastery.
